title: Business Planning Manager | 1 Year Contract | Central
description:
Lead development of business plans, strategies, and budgets to drive growth and profitability. Coordinate cross-functional teams to achieve goals.

Location: Tanjong Pagar

  • Collaborate in strategic business planning and budgeting exercises for Enterprises and Government. Contribute to syndication exercises with key product houses on a regular quarterly cadence, as well as the annual AOP cycle
  • Drive productive business targeting and administer seamless business operations for business through intelligence and insights from management reports, analysis, and data mining
  • Develop and prepare a forecasting model for regular business monitoring, including relevant sensitivity analysis and scenario planning. Ensure effectiveness and internal control of business unit operations by streamlining processes where necessary
  • Oversee essential projects as needed and conduct regular cadence reviews, such as funnel management, to ensure visibility of the business. Plan and coordinate interlocks with other business units to promote collaboration
  • Drive timely and accurate management reporting for key business decisions – Growth/BAU TMs and BPR’s and management dashboards and drivers
  • Ensure monthly month-end closing procedures are executed to ensure proper book closure
  • Stay informed of key trends affecting the business, including new developments in the enterprises and government sectors, progress of commercial enterprises, and market environment changes specific to certain vertical industries


Requirements:

  • University degree or equivalent in Accountancy, Finance & Business or related discipline
  • At least 3-5 years of experience in Business Planning / Analyst / Sales Operations
  • Demonstrated proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly in Excel and PowerPoint. Capable of managing and analyzing large volumes of data. Familiarity or experience with Salesforce, PowerBI and Tableau is preferred.
  • Experience in budgeting and driving accurate management reporting and analysis for key business decisions
  • Demands extensive strong cross-functional collaboration, good communication skills, and a process-focused aptitude that will enable this role to effectively execute multiple responsibilities and deliverables.
  • Strong business acumen with customer-oriented personal, independent and a good team player
  • Meticulous and responsible with sharp analytical, organizational, and multi-tasking skills
  • Ability to work well under stressful situations with tight timelines

Interested candidate please click "APPLY" to begin your job search journey and submit your CV directly through the official PERSOL job application platform - GO Mobile

We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

You acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree with the Privacy Policy.

postedCompany.description:
PERSOL is Asia-Pacific’s leading Staffing and HR solutions partner, operating across 13 markets with deep local insight and regional scale. With more than 80 offices and decades of experience, we deliver integrated workforce solutions that are tailored, tech-enabled, and designed for the dynamic world of work.

We combine human expertise with smart technology to help organisations solve workforce challenges, unlock potential, and stay ahead of change. From recruitment and talent management to workforce strategy and advisory, our collaborative approach puts your goals at the centre.

In 2025, we came together under the PERSOL name — reflecting our bold vision for the future of work and our Group’s Vision: Work and Smile.

Whether you’re building teams, growing careers, or transforming how work gets done, we’re here.
